reason: This finding involves modifying the GRUB2 bootloader configuration and generating a new grub.cfg. Any error in bootloader configuration or failure to correctly set the superuser/password can result in a system that is unbootable or inaccessible via the console, requiring physical/out-of-band recovery. Per policy, changes to the bootloader are classified as too dangerous to remediate automatically.
estimated_impact: reboot required; authentication/authorization affected; filesystems affected; Potential for system boot failure; Risk of losing console access to the bootloader; Requires manual verification of grub.cfg generation
